layer: Miss Lana / lead delivery outputs / Telegram + Google Sheets
description: Normalize and harden the working OUTPUT channels of the Miss Lana lead pipeline (Telegram owner group + Google Sheets) without changing the architecture, the acceptance rule, the form, or the durable schema. MongoDB Atlas stays the durable source of truth; Telegram and Google Sheets stay secondary and never participate in acceptance. The acceptance contract is unchanged: accepted = mongo.status === 'ok' || email.status === 'ok'.
scope: Deterministic, pure Telegram formatter (formatLeadTelegram): stable field order, omit empty optionals, event date keeps its calendar day, received time in America/Los_Angeles (PST/PDT), plain text (no parse_mode) safe for special chars, guaranteed under a safe length with only Notes truncated (marker added).

Fixed Google Sheets column contract (GOOGLE_SHEETS_COLUMNS) + hardened toSheetRow: stable cell count matching the header, null/undefined -> empty string, all strings, formula-injection neutralized, phone kept as text, full notes stored, submissionId + receivedAtUtc + receivedAtLosAngeles included.

Hardened appendToSheet: success only on 2xx AND JSON parsed AND body.ok === true; config-missing -> skipped; non-2xx / ok:false / malformed JSON / timeout / abort / network -> error; duplicate:true -> ok.

Hardened Google Apps Script (in the runbook): Script Properties for WEBHOOK_SECRET/SPREADSHEET_ID/SHEET_NAME, token auth, server-side submissionId dedupe under LockService, JSON {ok,duplicate,errorCode} responses, no PII/secret/stack in responses or logs.
Orchestration: a duplicate submissionId (Mongo idempotent insert) suppresses all secondary delivery (no second Telegram message / Sheet row / email) and returns a safe success.
notificationStatus carries the Google Sheets channel status (field `sheets`).
Tests: Telegram formatter, Sheets formatter, Sheets adapter hardening, idempotency/duplicate suppression, acceptance matrix incl. Sheets.
Docs: runbook, plan addendum, report, STATUS, PROJECT_PROGRESS, .env.example.
non_goals: Replace MongoDB or move the source of truth to Google Sheets.
Make Telegram or Google Sheets an acceptance channel.
Remove the email adapter or change the acceptance formula.
Redesign the form, add form fields, change pricing/SEO/domains, or change non-privacy public copy.
Build a CRM, admin dashboard, payment flow, or booking calendar.
Change Atlas/Vercel secrets, open the Google Sheet publicly, hard-code the webhook secret, or pass the secret in a URL query string.
Deploy, or run a live synthetic production test (requires explicit operator permission).
forbidden_execution_patterns: logging request/lead body, PII, or secrets

results.test:e2e: lead-api spec all green; site/design browser specs blocked by a Playwright browser-version mismatch in the sandbox (installed 1194, required 1223) — unrelated to this diff (server-side lib only)

mandatory_closure.verdict: DO NOT CLOSE (code/tests/docs CLOSE-ready; live verification gated on operator permission)
blockers_remaining: Step 9 controlled live verification (synthetic production + duplicate test) requires explicit operator permission and live secrets — not performed.
Privacy: Telegram chat membership + Google Sheet permissions are owner-controlled facts to confirm out-of-repo; retention period remains OPEN OWNER DECISION.
mandatory_closure.review_findings: No BLOCKER or unresolved HIGH. See report for full reviewer-grade pass.
